perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee at any time in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee only with prior written consent from the licensor, in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the conditions under which the Licensee can terminate the agreement. The original text states that Licensee can terminate 'at any time' if it attempts to assign or sublicense without consent. The modified text contradicts this by stating Licensee needs written consent from the licensor to terminate under those specific cicrumstances. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee only with prior written consent from the licensor, in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed Licensee to terminate at any time if it attempted to assign or sublicense without consent. The changed text, which is also the contradicted text in this case, now requires Licensee to obtain prior written consent from the Licensor in that same scenario to terminate. This introduces a direct contradiction because the Licensee's right to terminate 'at any time' is now restricted by the requirement of Licensor's consent. This is a significant inconsistency, making the terms unclear, and therefore constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion and shall not use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor, however the licensee, under special agreements can use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory, and the licensor will not have to approve or deny such use..
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the Licensee cannot use the Licensed Mark outside the Territory for any purpose, which requires the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. However, the modified text adds a condition that the licensee can use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory without needing to get the licensor approval for it, under special agreements. The introduced in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services that Investment Advisor may provide to Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor, however the licensee, under special agreements can use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory, and the licensor will not have to approve or deny such use. All rights not expressly granted to Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement. The original text states that the licensee cannot use the Licensed Mark outside the Territory for any purpose without the licensor's express written consent. The changed text adds an exception, allowing the licensee to use the Licensed Mark outside the Territory without licensor approval under special agreements. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the conditions under which the Licensed Mark can be used outside the Territory, thus triggering an in-text contradiction as described in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall immediately notify the other party and provide to the other party all relevant background facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement"), or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim").
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall immediately notify the other party and provide to the other party all relevant background facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement"), however if the licensee is the one misusing it or infringing it, no notification is necessary , or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim").
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that each party must immediately notify the other party of any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party. However, the modified text adds an exception: if the licensee is the one misusing it or infringing it, no notification is necessary. The introduced in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notification of Infringement. Each party shall immediately notify the other party and provide to the other party all relevant background facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuse of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement"), however if the licensee is the one misusing it or infringing it, no notification is necessary , or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim"). Licensor shall have the exclusive right, but not the obligation, to prosecute, defend and/or settle in its sole discretion, all actions, proceedings and claims involving any Third Party Infringement or Third Party Claim, and to take any other action that it deems necessary or proper for the protection and preservation of its rights in the Licensed Mark. Licensee shall cooperate with Licensor in the prosecution, defense or settlement of such actions, proceedings or claims.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the notification requirement, stating that no notification is necessary if the licensee is the one misusing or infringing the Licensed Mark. This directly contradicts the original broad requirement that each party must notify the other of *any* infringements or misuse, creating an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text is the same as the original and includes the modification. The modified clause creates uncertainty about when notification is required and undermines the licensor's ability to protect its rights in the Licensed Mark.
